senators.name: Michael Bennet
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Wesleyan and J.D. from Yale Law School, where he was editor-in-chief of the Yale Law Journal — a marker of exceptional analytical rigor. As superintendent of Denver Public Schools, led data-driven education reform. Known in the Senate for deep, methodical policy work on education, tax, and child poverty. Consistently cites research and data in floor speeches.
education: J.D., Yale Law School (Editor-in-Chief, Yale Law Journal)
experience: Chief of Staff to Denver Mayor Hickenlooper

senators.name: John Hickenlooper
senators.nerdReason: M.S. in geology from Wesleyan University. Worked as a petroleum geologist in Colorado before pivoting to entrepreneurship. Science education gives him genuine analytical grounding. As governor, known for pragmatic, data-informed approach to energy and economic policy. Brings a scientist's perspective to Senate Commerce and Energy committees.

CO-01.name: Diana DeGette
CO-01.nerdReason: B.A. from Colorado College and J.D. from NYU School of Law. Decades-long champion of stem cell research and evidence-based health policy on the House Energy and Commerce Committee. Has been a driving force behind science-based medical research funding and FDA reform. Her sustained focus on research policy elevates her above typical non-STEM legislators.
education: J.D., New York University School of Law

CO-02.name: Joe Neguse
CO-02.nerdReason: B.S. in political science and economics summa cum laude from CU Boulder and J.D. from CU Law. Founded a civic engagement organization as a student. Youngest-ever member of CU Board of Regents. Served as executive director of Colorado DORA (regulatory agencies), demonstrating deep analytical and regulatory expertise. Now House Assistant Democratic Leader.
education: J.D., University of Colorado School of Law
B.S. Political Science and Economics, University of Colorado Boulder (summa cum laude)
experience: Executive Director, Colorado Dept. of Regulatory Agencies
University of Colorado Board of Regents

CO-03.name: Jeff Hurd
CO-03.nerdReason: B.A. in classical literature and philosophy from Notre Dame, J.D. from University of Denver, and LL.M. from Columbia Law School. Deeply educated with three post-secondary degrees. Specializes in complex rural utility and energy cooperative law — a technical niche requiring analytical depth. Clerked for a federal appeals court judge.
education: J.D., University of Denver, Sturm College of Law
B.A. Liberal Studies (Classical Literature and Philosophy), University of Notre Dame
experience: Attorney specializing in rural electric cooperatives
Law Clerk, U.S. Court of Appeals, 10th Circuit

CO-04.name: Lauren Boebert
CO-04.notNerdReason: GED education with no college degree. Career in restaurant ownership and natural gas industry before Congress. No demonstrated analytical, research-based, or evidence-driven approach to policy. Legislative focus driven by ideology rather than data or expertise.

CO-05.name: Jeff Crank
CO-05.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from Colorado State University. Career in congressional staffing, Chamber of Commerce advocacy, conservative political organization (Americans for Prosperity COO), and radio broadcasting. Policy positions driven by political philosophy rather than analytical or evidence-based frameworks.
education: B.A. Political Science, Colorado State University
experience: Senior VP, Colorado Springs Chamber of Commerce
Legislative Assistant to Rep. Joel Hefley

CO-06.name: Jason Crow
CO-06.nerdReason: B.A. from UW-Madison and J.D. from University of Denver. Army Ranger (Bronze Star, three tours) who brings analytical military intelligence and strategic thinking to Congress. Serves on Intelligence and Armed Services Committees. Pro-bono Lawyer of the Year for work with veterans. Combines rigorous legal training with disciplined military analytical mindset.
education: J.D., University of Denver, Sturm College of Law
experience: U.S. Army Ranger, Captain (82nd Airborne, 75th Ranger Regiment)
Denver Pro-bono Lawyer of the Year

CO-07.name: Brittany Pettersen
CO-07.notNerdReason: B.A. in political science from Metropolitan State University of Denver. First-generation college graduate. Career in community organizing, advocacy, and state legislature. Passionate policy advocate but approach is driven by personal experience and activism rather than analytical or research-based frameworks.
education: B.A. Political Science, Metropolitan State University of Denver

CO-08.name: Gabe Evans
CO-08.notNerdReason: B.A. in government from Patrick Henry College. Career as Army officer (Captain) and police lieutenant. Military and law enforcement backgrounds require discipline and structured thinking but not analytical or research-driven policy approach.
education: B.A. Government, Patrick Henry College (cum laude)
experience: U.S. Army, Captain (deployed to Middle East)
